issues.title: WSL 2 mirrored networking breaks NFS mounts (“Connection timed out”)
issues.next_action_reason: Old high-reach networking debt with a community-verified workaround and no team acknowledgment; post the summary + add workaround-available, then file the reserved-port root-cause follow-up.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks all for the detailed logs and for narrowing this down. To summarize for anyone hitting this: under mirrored networking, adding `noresvport` to the NFS mount options (or the equivalent in docker-compose) restores connectivity because mirrored mode does not preserve the reserved source port NFS expects. We'll track the underlying reserved-port behavior as a mirrored-networking bug; in the meantime `noresvport` is the recommended workaround. If anyone still sees timeouts *with* `noresvport` set, please attach fresh `wsl --debug-shell` networking logs and your `.wslconfig`.

issues.title: [Help wanted] Default window decorations color

issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for keeping this alive — it stays one of the most-requested decoration issues. To summarize where it stands for newcomers: WSLg draws server-side (RDP RAIL) decorations for the title bar, so an app's own GTK_THEME does not control the titlebar color; apps that draw client-side decorations (many GTK apps) follow the theme, while apps that rely on the WM/server titlebar (JetBrains, xcalc) get the default light frame. This is tagged help-wanted — a PR that plumbs a dark-titlebar preference (or honors the host's dark-mode / a `.wslgconfig` setting) through the RAIL decoration path would be very welcome. If anyone investigating wants pointers on where the titlebar color is chosen in the compositor, reply here and we can help scope it.
